items.name: Biology 101
items.description:
Important for medical or other science related fields.

To start the class, pay for it and include it to be subtracted for the daily recipe to gain hours, like how Math 101 is set up.

The class will be taught over 1 week, and you'll need to attend most of the classes to get the best grades.

items.name: Calendar
items.description: Tells the day of the week. Must be marked by hand.

items.name: Cash Register
items.description: To work as a cashier (behind the counter) or bagger (at the end of the counter).

items.name: Days Count
items.description: Number of days since beginning the game.

items.name: Energy
items.description:
Requires daily rest and food.

10 is full alertness, focus, and strength. Energy reserves should go higher than that at the end of each day, to prepare for the next. Reserves won't make you feel better, though.

items.name: Hours
items.description:
The basic measure of time needed to accomplish things. Starts (with 24 hours left) at sunrise — the hours aren't counted from midnight, even when staying up all night.

To conserve time (by sleeping less) and food, you could lower the energy required to start the next day. But you won't be at peak performance, and if too low, that might mean lying in bed all day.

Could make sense if your character is too sick to keep food down. Hopefully, you don't let them get depressed and avoid food, sleep, and work.

items.name: Math 101
items.description:
This is a good time to decide whether you're happy enough to study strange symbols like capital greek letter sigma and elongated S's, or whether you'll switch to Art or another major to avoid further punishment.

The class will be taught over 1 week, and you'll need to attend most of the classes to get the best grades.

items.name: Math 101 Homework
items.description: Improves your chances on the final test.

items.name: Math 101 Taken
items.description: Hopefully you will attend class each day. This is your record of attendance. If it's too low, you're sure to get a poor or failing grade. You can attend 1 hour each day, while the class lasts.

items.name: Meal
items.description: One meal a day should be plenty. Healthier (producing fuller energy) if cooked rather than eaten straight out of a box. You could prepare several portions and refrigerate them.

items.name: Money
items.description: Pay for classes, rent, buy food and clothing.

items.name: Park Facilities
items.description: Places you find at the park.

items.name: Party
items.description: Reduces stress and increases people skills but can significantly reduce intelligence, especially if you behave wildly.

items.name: Pre-Med
items.description:
This degree isn't designed as an end, but rather, a means to eventually finish an M.D. and become a physician. If you're not interested in that much school, then it would be better to choose a different major.

Undergrad degrees at this school typically require 8 classes taken. So, to finish this degree, add 6 more as sources for the recipe.

items.name: Stress
items.description: Byproduct of certain actions. Don't let it build up too high. The best way to blow off steam is to hang out at the park with friends.

items.name: Student Loan
items.description: If you take out a loan now, monthly payments will be required later. Interest on these loans is not too bad, however, as the government encourages people to get college degrees.

locations.name: Greek House
locations.description: Fraternities and Sororities have the wildest parties.

locations.name: Grocery Store
locations.description:
Buy either unprepared food or boxed meals.

There are several types of jobs available here:
• Cashier
• Bagger
• Stocker
• Manager

locations.name: Home
locations.description:
An adequate apartment. Could buy a nice house with mortgage payment, otherwise rent.

Sleep and cook meals here.

locations.name: Park
locations.description: Good place to hang out and reduce stress. Trees, basketball courts, skateboarding.

locations.name: Restaurant
locations.description: More expensive than the grocery store but doesn't require preparation and more filling than boxed food.

locations.name: School
locations.description: Full time is generally considered to be 2 classes, although classes vary as to how demanding they are. You could push and go up to 3, or take only 1 for a part-time load.

attribs.name: Grade
attribs.description: Letter grade for the class, based primarily on the final test. Low attendance also reduces grades.

attribs.name: Intelligence
attribs.description:
Improves study and job performance.

Spending time on things that improve intelligence, such as studying, typically lowers people skills.

attribs.name: People Skills
attribs.description:
Being cool, popular, or a good listener is more important for some jobs than how much you know. And it's always useful to help with finding a job, and in interviews.

Spending time on things that improve people skills typically lowers intelligence.

attribs.name: Score
attribs.description: Percentage on the final test. Studying improves your chances.

events.name: Final Test
events.description:
Main factor to determine class grade.

There's some possible variation on outcomes, but mostly it's determined by studying. Based on time spent doing homework, add up to 80 for stat adjustment.

overall settings.title: Making a Living
overall settings.description:
Attend class or go to work. Figure out *what* you want to do with your life; then, figure out *how* to do that. Or maybe the other way around. In this scenario, resources such as time and money are tightly controlled, so {CadetBlue|spend them wisely}.

You've just enrolled in the first class, Math 101, which starts tomorrow morning. It counts as credit towards a variety of different degrees. Arrangements have also been made to continue working part time at a grocery store in the afternoons and evenings.
